heroTitle: Learn to work with medicinal plants
heroDesc: Practical workshops in small groups — accessible to beginners and the more experienced curious alike. Discover how to identify, prepare and use plants for your health, at home.
w1Desc: What is herbal medicine? How do plants act on the body? What are the different preparations — teas, tinctures, balms? A first step into the world of medicinal plants.
w2Desc: The plant allies of the female cycle, fertility, PCOS, endometriosis and perimenopause. How to identify them, use them safely, and build a simple daily protocol.
w3Desc: Learn to formulate your own blends — herbal teas, infused oils, plant body butters. You leave with your creations and the knowledge to continue at home.
w4Desc: What is an adaptogen and why do we need them today? Ashwagandha, rhodiola, eleuthero, tulsi — learn to know them, choose them and use them with discernment.
philosophyTitle: Knowledge that lives in your hands, not just your head
philosophyDesc1: There is a version of wellness education that fills you with information and sends you away with a handout. That is not what I am trying to do.
philosophyDesc2: Every workshop I design starts from a single question: what will this person be able to do differently when they leave? Groups are small — never more than twelve — because learning about your own health deserves attention.
w1Learns: Make an effective herbal infusion and decoction
Prepare a simple tincture and infused oil at home
Choose the right preparation method for each herb
Build a small home herbal kit for seasonal and everyday use
Know which herbs are safe and what to be careful with
w2Learns: How the hormonal system works and where herbs interact with it
Key herbs for endometriosis, PCOS, PMS and perimenopause
The adrenal-hormonal connection and how to support it
Naturopathic foundations — diet, lifestyle, what actually matters
When to work with a practitioner and what to expect
w3Learns: The knowledge to adjust and remake your blends at home
Safe extraction ratios for tinctures and infused oils
How to store, label and use what you make
A personalised blend designed for your specific needs
w4Learns: The difference between adaptogens, nervines and tonics
How to choose the right adaptogen for your stress pattern
Contraindications and what to watch for
Simple daily protocols you can actually maintain
formatDesc1: Workshops run in small groups — typically 6 to 12 people — to allow genuine exchange and individual questions.
formatDesc2: Some workshops are offered in person in London, others online via Zoom. Make Your Own Preparations requires physical attendance.
formatDesc3: All workshops are available in English and French. Groups can be organised in either language depending on attendees.
forWhoItems: Complete beginners with no prior experience
People who want to complement their individual consultation
Groups of friends, wellness clubs, associations
Health professionals wanting to deepen their knowledge of herbal medicine
Companies looking for original wellness formats for their teams
